zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood於1403年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾卡爾德武德時，他的父親Sir Robert Maxwell of Calderwood30歲，母親Elizabeth Danielstoun25歲。他和Margaret Borthwick至少有1個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1476年6月6日在蘇格蘭雷福福爾波特 格拉斯哥享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: Maxwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from a place in Kelso, near Melrose in Roxburghshire, the name of a salmon pool on the Tweed near Kelso Bridge. The placename is first recorded in 1144 in the form Mackeswell ‘Mack's spring or stream’ (Old English well(a)).
Irish: adopted for Ó Meisceall, see Miskell . This surname is common in Ulster.
Jewish: arbitrary adoption of the Scottish name, or Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Miskell
meanings: Irish (Galway): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Meisceall ‘descendant of Meiscill’, a personal name of unexplained etymology. Sometimes Anglicized as Maxwell .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Calderwood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Calderwood in Lanarkshire, named from the Cumbrian river name Calder (see Calder ) + Middle English wode ‘wood’.

variants.name: Calder
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of the places called Calder in Midlothian and Caithness, or Cawdor in Nairnshire.

English: perhaps a habitational name from Calder in Cumbria, named from the river on which it stands (probably a British name, from Welsh caled ‘hard, violent’ + dwfr ‘water, stream’). However, the modern surname in England seems to be of Scottish origin, rather than from the Cumbrian placename.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood was born in 1403, in Calderwood, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, Sir Robert Maxwell of Calderwood, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Danielstoun, was 25. He had at least 1 son and 3 daughters with Margaret Borthwick. He died on 6 June 1476, in Port Glasgow, Renfrewshire, Scotland, at the age of 73.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood(이)가 1403년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 캘더우드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Maxwell of Calderwood(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Danielstoun(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Margaret Borthwick(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1476년 6월 6일 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 포트 글라스고에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Maxwell of Calderwood родился в 1403 г. в местности калдирвуд, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Maxwell of Calderwood было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Danielstoun, 25.
